title: Causal Latent Tyre-Wear Filter
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets and uncertainty, which is closer to race reality than fixed expected-life constants.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models wear as a latent state with pit-stop resets and uncertainty, which is closer to race reality than fixed expected-life constants. Official F1 strategy material also stresses that degradation and fresh-tyre outlap pace are what make undercuts and pit timing work.
implementation_hint: Within each Driver x Race x Stint sequence, fit a causal local-linear or Kalman-style filter on lap-time residuals after race/compound normalization, resetting state on PitStop. Feed filtered wear level, wear slope, wear acceleration, one-step forecast error, and uncertainty width into the existing tabular stack; never use future laps.
expected_effect: Better detection of pre-pit cliff laps that look similar on raw TyreLife but differ in latent pace decay.
risk: Sensitive to safety-car, wet, and traffic outliers; any non-causal residualization will leak badly.
